runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 8 in a U.S. microsimulation model under current-law tax-benefit rules.
citations: Saez, Slemrod, and Giertz (2012) elasticity review
Gruber and Saez (2002) taxable income responsiveness
Auten, Carroll, and Hines (2002) labor supply / taxable income response evidence
runs.reasoningSummary: I think decile-8 primary earners have a modest but clearly positive substitution response, smaller than extensive-margin groups and somewhat below headline taxable-income elasticities. Current-law microsimulation settings usually imply a midrange value around 0.2, with uncertainty spanning roughly 0.1 to 0.4 and an upper tail below 1.
